summary: Decompiled five item/Pokemon-related functions across Blastoise/Kamex, Kirby Cutter beam, Lugia, Goomba/Oldkuri, and Pikachu Thunder. The PR replaced placeholder markers with matching C implementations, tightened several function prototypes in headers, and added required item/database includes for spawn and callback logic. The PR text states all five functions were verified as 100% matching by an overnight script.
what_changed: Implemented `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim`, replacing its placeholder with animation/update logic for Kamex while its Pokemon timer and accessory callbacks are active.
Implemented `it_8029BAB8` in `itkirbycutterbeam.c` to spawn the Kirby Cutter beam item using a populated `SpawnItem`, parent links, facing direction, zeroed velocity, and post-spawn setup calls.
Updated `itkirbycutterbeam.h` so `it_8029BB90` has a concrete signature: `void it_8029BB90(Item_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* owner)` instead of `UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS`.

Implemented `it_802D1E8C` in `itlugia.c` as a helper that creates an item of a supplied `ItemKind` using Lugia item state, position helper `it_8026BB88`, velocity helper `it_802D1F64`, owner fields, and `Item_80268B18`.
Updated `itlugia.h` to give concrete prototypes for `it_802D1E8C(Item_GObj*, ItemKind, f32)` and `it_802D1F64(Item_GObj*, f32)` returning `Item_GObj*` and `Vec3` respectively.

Implemented `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`, handling Oldkuri rolling physics by applying stored horizontal velocity, reacting to `xDFC` and `it_802750E8`, changing animation state, projecting velocity against floor normal, and applying item physics.

Implemented `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll`, handling Pikachu Thunder collision branches, positional offset checks, callback into Pikachu down-special helper functions, and terminating via `it_802B22B8` in collision/end conditions.
Added required includes such as `db/db.h`, `it/item.h`, `it/types.h`, and `it/it_266F.h` where the new decompiled code depends on concrete item, spawn, collision, or debug helper declarations.
smart_moves: Converted adjacent unknown header prototypes while decompiling callers/callees, reducing future `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` usage and making item spawn ownership flow clearer.

Used `SpawnItem` initialization patterns already present in the item system: set `kind`, `prev_pos`, `pos`, `facing_dir`, parent gobj fields, damage, velocity, flags, and `x40` before calling `Item_80268B18`.
Preserved matching-sensitive local structure, including repeated `GET_ITEM` locals and `PAD_STACK(8)` in functions where stack layout mattered.
Kept item-specific state accesses in the existing `xDD4_itemVar.<item>` layout rather than inventing new abstractions, which is appropriate for matching item code.
Hooked spawned Kirby Cutter beam into existing setup helpers after allocation: `it_8029BB90`, `db_80225DD8`, and `it_802750F8`, only when the spawn returned non-null.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling item spawn helpers, concrete `SpawnItem` field ordering and parent relationships are central to matching and to documenting behavior.
Header cleanup is often a useful side effect of matching a function: once call signatures are proven by the implementation, replace `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` with concrete types.
Pokemon/item motion functions often require matching-sensitive redundant locals, manual timer resets, and callback assignments such as `entered_hitlag`, `exited_hitlag`, and `on_accessory`.

Collision callbacks can include fighter-specific side effects; here Pikachu Thunder collision checks call `ftPk_SpecialLw_CheckProperty` and `ftPk_SpecialLw_80127608` through a stored fighter-related pointer.
Physics functions may combine item state variables, floor normals, and generic item physics helpers, as seen in Oldkuri using `it_8027C8D0` and `it_8027C0A8` after restoring horizontal velocity.
Evidence for exact behavioral naming is limited by remaining unknown field names such as `xDD4_itemVar.*`, `xDFC`, `xDF4`, `x38`, and `x70_pad`; avoid over-renaming these without additional context.
naming_conventions: Existing item motion naming is retained: `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll`.
Unknown-address functions remain in address-style names when no semantic name is proven, e.g. `it_8029BAB8` and `it_802D1E8C`.
Concrete typedefs and project item types are preferred once known: `Item_GObj*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `ItemKind`, `Vec3`, and `f32`.
Item-specific attributes follow existing naming like `itKamexAttributes`, `itOldkuriAttributes`, and `itPikachuthunderAttributes`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll`, indicating stack-padding was needed for matching.
Introduced inner-scope repeated `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)` locals in Kamex and Oldkuri functions, a common tactic when compiler register allocation or stack layout requires a fresh local.
Preserved explicit zero assignments for vector components and `SpawnItem` fields instead of relying on aggregate initialization, matching the original codegen style.
Used direct struct field assignments to `spawn.vel.x`, `spawn.vel.y`, `spawn.vel.z`, `spawn.x44_flag.b0`, and parent fields to reproduce item spawning assembly.
The PR body reports all five functions were 100% matched and verified by an overnight script.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human-text evidence beyond the diff is the PR summary and verification note stating all five functions matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type fields in `xDD4_itemVar.pokemon`, `xDD4_itemVar.oldkuri`, `xDD4_itemVar.lugia`, or `xDD4_itemVar.pikachuthunder`.
Search for other `SpawnItem` decompilations using `Item_80268B18` to compare parent field and flag initialization patterns.
Look for follow-up work on `it_802D1F64` and `it_802D208C` in Lugia to complete the nearby helper chain.
Search for additional replacements of `UNK_RET it_8029BB90(UNK_PARAMS)` or callers of the newly typed `it_8029BB90(Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*)`.
Investigate whether `itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim` timer behavior later receives semantic names for `timer`, `x68`, and `x6C`.
